svg.text: Navigation Flows: Every Path Through the Knowledge Space
Flow 1: Hierarchical Drill-Down (the map)
Mission Map → click "Self-Healing" → Mission Document → click "CAP-HEALTH-ENDPOINT" → Capability Document
→ click "REQ-HEALTH-001" → Requirement Document (EARS + GWT) → click "implement-health-endpoint" → Task Detail (DNA)
4 clicks: mission to task
Flow 2: Breadcrumb Up (back to context)
On Task Detail page → breadcrumb: Self-Healing > CAP-HEALTH-ENDPOINT > REQ-HEALTH-001 > [this task]
Click "Self-Healing" → jump directly to mission. Click "CAP-HEALTH-ENDPOINT" → jump to capability. Any level, one click.
Flow 3: Cross-Reference Jump (sideways)
On CAP-AUTH page → cross-refs bar: "Also composed by: Fair Attribution | Structured Knowledge Objects"
Click "Structured Knowledge Objects" → jump to that mission → see how AUTH is used in a different context.
Graph traversal, not tree
Flow 4: Search Warp Drive (jump anywhere)
On ANY page → type in search bar: "how do agents handle JWT" → Results:
[REQ] REQ-AUTH-001 (0.91) | [CAP] CAP-AUTH (0.87) | [TASK] brain-identity-auth (0.84) | [THOUGHT] JWT session design (0.78)
Any level, any language
Flow 5: Agent Context Recovery (bottom-up)
Agent claims task "implement-health-endpoint" → reads DNA → needs more context → follows requirement_ref to REQ-HEALTH-001
→ reads EARS spec + GWT examples → follows capability_id to CAP-HEALTH-ENDPOINT → reads architecture → understands WHY
Context recovery WITHOUT brain
Flow 6: Release Management (human approval)
Mission Document → Releases tab → Phase 1 backlog (6 tasks listed) → human reviews scope → clicks "Release Phase 1"
→ 6 backlog tasks move to pending → pipeline starts → agents pick up work via A2A SSE
Flow 7: Version History (time travel)
Capability page → Version History section → click v0.0.1 → see what CAP-AUTH looked like before brain identity was added
